channel.title: The Grant Desk - Remote
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Remote.

item.title: Global Artivism: Governance Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,200 to 1,800 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting a Governance Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on governance systems, democratic institutional design, and Global Majority-led movement infrastructure. The contractor supports the development and maintenance of Global Artivism's governance architecture, including documentation, committee coordination, and research that supports the Democratic Transition process, and is expected to take initiative on assigned tasks and grow through structured mentorship. The movement is governed by a Global Council with a Movement Secretariat coordination team; the Fellow coordinates primarily with the Governance Lead alongside the Operations Director, Secretariat members, and Council committees (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: governance documentation (frameworks, committee charters, terms of reference, process documentation; maintaining an organized, current, consistently formatted document library; preparing materials for Global Council meetings); committee coordination (scheduling, materials, meeting notes, follow-up, trackers of decisions and action items, agendas and briefing packs); Democratic Transition and electoral process support (research on governance models, electoral processes, and democratic participation mechanisms; drafting materials for the Democratic Transition Roadmap 2025-2027; preparing electoral framework and testing documentation; tracking milestones); and governance research (comparative analyses and briefing notes; staying current on distributed governance, participatory democracy, and decentralized leadership; contributing to knowledge products). REQUIRED: manages multiple documentation tasks and meets deadlines with attention to accuracy and formatting (experience via union organizing, participatory facilitation, cooperative governance, or community-based decision-making counts alongside academic or formal work history); good written English; ability to take initiative and work independently; genuine interest in governance, movement building, or institutional design; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in governance, law, political science, or international development; interest in Global Majority leadership and distributed power; civil society/NGO/movement experience; familiarity with arts, culture, or social justice movements; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Operations Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,000 to 1,500 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting an Operations Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on organizational infrastructure and systems design. Working alongside the Operations Director, the contractor designs operational frameworks spanning financial workflows, contractor management, governance alignment, and onboarding, contributing directly to infrastructure decisions while building practical experience in how a Global Majority-led movement organization functions operationally. The Fellow coordinates primarily with the Operations Director alongside the Governance Lead, Project Manager, Admin Coordinator, and Secretariat members (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority; decision-making is distributed through governance structures and committees). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: infrastructure design and framework development (designing and documenting operational frameworks; contributing to staffing models, organizational structures, and resource frameworks that enable both centralized and decentralized work; designing onboarding and operational guidance; aligning operational systems with governance frameworks and democratic decision-making); systems and knowledge documentation (operational guidance and toolkits for distributed teams; internal knowledge systems such as documentation, checklists, and decision trees); and capacity building and research (identifying where documentation or systems redesign enables teams to work more independently; flagging operational bottlenecks and proposing solutions; sharing learning with Council, committees, and teams; researching operational models relevant to Global Artivism's structure). REQUIRED: manages multiple tasks and meets deadlines with attention to detail and comfort coordinating logistics across a distributed team (experience via coordinating community events, grassroots logistics, or operations in any context counts alongside formal work history); good written English; comfort with digital tools (Google Workspace, Slack, or similar); genuine interest in operations, organizational systems, or movement infrastructure; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in business administration, project management, or finance; interest in international civil society, movement building, or Global Majority leadership; civil society/NGO/community experience; familiarity with digital project management or operations tools; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Manifund: AI Safety Regranting (Rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Manifund

Location: Remote; US-based platform, open worldwide.

Award: Grants typically USD 5,000 to 50,000, recommended by independent regrantors from their own budgets. No application fee. No legal entity required.

Duration: Varies by project (rolling)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Manifund's regranting program funds AI safety and global-catastrophic-risk work through a network of expert regrantors, each given an independent budget to recommend grants to people and projects they find promising. Applicants submit a project proposal on the Manifund platform; regrantors can then offer funding. GRANTS: typically USD 5,000 to 50,000, though amounts vary. ELIGIBILITY: individuals (no legal entity or institutional affiliation required) and organizations working on AI safety or reducing catastrophic risk from AI. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis; per-regrantor budget availability varies over time. Learn more at the regranting page and apply on the Manifund platform (manifund.org).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Paid Research Assistant: AI and the War in Ukraine
item.description:
Organisation: Bogna Konior (NYU Shanghai)

Location: Remote-friendly.

Award: USD 18 per hour; 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule.

Duration: Ongoing part-time research assistantship; start as soon as possible.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Bogna Konior, Assistant Professor of Media Theory at NYU Shanghai, is hiring a paid research assistant for a project on Ukraine and artificial intelligence. The work supports a forthcoming book with MIT Press, plus an independent scenario she is writing on the future of AI with the war as a lens. WHAT THE WORK LOOKS LIKE: going month-by-month through news archives (New York Times, Reuters, Financial Times, and specialist outlets), annotating and cataloguing coverage of AI, drones, and autonomous systems in the war, plus other developments she will direct you to; building and maintaining a structured Notion database covering chronology, companies, industries, and key actors; and organizing and editing her existing notes. WHO SHE IS LOOKING FOR: interest in and general knowledge of AI; prior research and annotation experience, careful and detail-oriented, no sloppy citations; comfortable with Notion and with AI tools; Ukrainian language skill a strong plus (not required); familiarity with AI, war, tech, and geopolitics news sources is great. Remote-friendly, start ASAP. She will also consider people with no prior experience, as well as current and previous students. TERMS: USD 18 per hour, 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule. TO APPLY: email bogna@nyu.edu with a short introduction and background (link to website, socials, CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio if you have one); where you are based and your weekly availability; and one recent AI-related story, company, or development you find interesting and why (a vibe check). Bonus: a sample of past research or writing if you have it.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Padimai: OOPS Onchain Artist Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Padimai Art and Tech Studio

Location: Remote residency (Padimai is based in Singapore); open internationally.

Award: SGD 15,000 honorarium per artist plus an additional SGD 5,000 per artist for documentation (audio, video, photography). Five practitioners selected. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months (20 September to 20 December 2026)

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

OOPS (Origin Of Primary Source) is the first edition of Padimai Art and Tech Studio's Onchain Artist Residency, a three-month remote residency inviting art practitioners to critically and creatively engage with NFTs as artistic registers and as systems of knowledge, documentation, and access. Five practitioners are selected. SUPPORT: SGD 15,000 honorarium per artist, plus SGD 5,000 per artist for documentation. The residency is remote, with community engagement designed through online meetings; at the end, participants deliver three NFTs developed during the residency to Padimai. ELIGIBILITY: individual creative practitioners working at the intersection of art, technology, and blockchain; open internationally. RESIDENCY: 20 September to 20 December 2026. DEADLINE: 15 August 2026. Apply via the residency page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RYSE Innovation Hub: Youth Innovation Advisor
item.description:
Organisation: RYSE Innovation Hub

Location: 100% remote.

Award: USD 480 monthly compensation. No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027; up to 4 hours per week; 100% remote.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

RYSE Innovation Hub seeks Youth Innovation Advisors: young people ages 18 to 24 who are passionate about adolescent health and wellness. Youth Innovation Advisors support projects that strengthen youth engagement practices and help shape health solutions created with young people in mind. The role is 100% remote, running from September 2026 to July 2027, with a time commitment of up to 4 hours per week. WHO IT'S FOR: young people ages 18 to 24 passionate about adolescent health and wellness. COMPENSATION: USD 480 monthly. DEADLINE: 20 July 2026, 11:59 PM ET. TO APPLY: submit the application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL)

Location: Remote; international organisation. Experience working in or with global majority countries encouraged.

Award: USD 5,000 stipend for a 6-8 month fellowship. No application fee.

Duration: 6-8 month research fellowship; rolling applications

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship supports curious, passionate mid-career researchers and practitioners to explore frontier questions on digital public infrastructure (DPI) and data governance. Fellows bring their own knowledge and research agenda while contributing to DIAL's core thematic priorities: creating more value for more people from data; understanding how digital systems and services impact people, including and especially the most marginalised; and accelerating the adoption of data-sharing and governance practices that fuel innovation and empower people. SUPPORT: a USD 5,000 stipend for research completed over 6-8 months, plus mentorship from DIAL staff and industry experts, access to DIAL's network and resources, collaboration with partner organisations, and support amplifying the work. Each fellow produces two preliminary pieces and one final, more extensive output (research papers or reports, policy briefs, expert-comment series, etc.). ELIGIBILITY: professionals from academia, the private sector, and civil society, typically with 8-15 years of experience in digital development, policy, or research; demonstrated work at the intersection of technology and society (including DPI and data governance); a track record of high-quality writing, research, or policy briefs; experience working in or with global majority countries; and strong analytical and communication skills. TO APPLY (rolling basis): submit a CV or resume, a writing sample or portfolio, and a research proposal. Questions: fellows@dial.global. Apply via https://dial.global/apply-to-our-fellows-program/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Humans in Control: Distributed Organizing Manager (Remote)
item.description:
Organisation: Humans in Control

Location: Remote (United States; US work authorization required)

Award: USD 65,000 to 80,000 per year, plus benefits (medical, dental, vision, PTO, 401(k)). No application fee.

Duration: Full-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Humans in Control empowers the public to shape AI policy through grassroots organizing and bipartisan advocacy for commonsense safeguards that protect children and families. The Distributed Organizing Manager builds and supports distributed/volunteer organizing to advance the organization's advocacy goals. This is a full-time, fully remote role open to candidates anywhere in the United States (US work authorization required). Compensation is USD 65,000-80,000 plus benefits. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis (no fixed deadline). See the listing for full responsibilities and to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Principles of Intelligence: Research Scientist (Ambitious Mechanistic Interpretability)
item.description:
Organisation: Principles of Intelligence (PrincInt)

Location: Fully remote (strong preference for candidates in or able to spend time in London, UK and/or the Bay Area, US)

Award: Estimated salary USD 100,000 to USD 250,000 gross, depending on location and experience. Benefits include 4 weeks paid holiday plus additional time off and flexible hours. Visa sponsorship possible for candidates based in or relocating to London (UK) or Berkeley/Bay Area (US). No application fee.

Duration: Full-time (or contractor); start date July 2026

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

Principles of Intelligence (PrincInt) is hiring Research Scientists to work on Ambitious Mechanistic Interpretability (AMI): developing tractable, realistic, scale-aware data structure models; quantifying the relationship between data structure and the features AI systems learn; and building synthetic datasets to benchmark and improve interpretability tools. The team is currently exploring a data model based on high-dimensional percolation theory (a statistically self-similar, sparse structure), working at the intersection of physics and mechanistic interpretability. The role is FULLY REMOTE and async. MUST HAVE: a PhD or equivalent research experience in a technical field (math, physics, neuroscience, computer science, or complexity science); ability to communicate complex novel ideas; working knowledge of neural networks and deep learning; experience with scientific computing in Python; comfort in a fully remote environment; and strong motivation to contribute to AI safety. GREAT TO HAVE: a publication record in ML or a quantitative field; knowledge of statistical physics (percolation theory, renormalization), mechanistic interpretability, stochastic/coalescent processes, combinatorics, or network science; experience with fractal geometry, neural scaling laws, graph/tree algorithms, or theories of concepts in cognitive science; or familiarity with the ARENA curriculum. The team meets in person 2-4 times a year. PROCESS: application, screening call, remote interview, research talk, a paid one-day remote work trial, and references. Apply by 21 June 2026; applications reviewed on a rolling basis. Accommodations: info@princint.ai.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Horizon: AI Policy Career Accelerator - Cohort 3 (2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service

Location: Remote

Award: Up to $100,000 in career-development funding per participant (for unpaid or underpaid placements, courses, conferences, relocation and other career costs), plus 1-on-1 mentorship, dedicated training, application coaching, hiring-manager introductions and exclusive job listings. No application fee.

Duration: 9-month part-time programme (typically under 2 hrs/week outside an optional 4-6 week training period)

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

Horizon's 9-month part-time, remote AI Policy Career Accelerator helps people from a wide range of backgrounds move into AI governance and policy roles in Congress, agencies and think tanks. Cohort 3 participants receive 1-on-1 mentorship from experienced policy practitioners, dedicated training on working in Congress / agencies / think tanks, coaching on resumes, cover letters and writing samples, direct introductions to hiring managers, and access to exclusive (non-public) job listings. Up to $100,000 is available in career-development funding per participant for unpaid or underpaid roles, courses, conferences, relocation and more. ELIGIBILITY: open to all backgrounds and experience levels (students, mid-career professionals, academics, researchers, lawyers, engineers); no prior policy experience required. Time commitment is flexible (typically under 2 hours/week outside an optional 4-6 week training period). Applications close 21 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: David McClure Public Interest Journalism Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Oxford International Centre for Publishing, Oxford Brookes University

Location: Fully remote

Award: GBP 15,000 grant to support one nonfiction writing fellow. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship starts each September (funding for one fellow per year for five years)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Fellowship from the Oxford International Centre for Publishing at Oxford Brookes University, supporting one nonfiction writer per year (for the next five years) with a GBP 15,000 award to work on a timely, independent piece of long-form public interest journalism that might otherwise never be published. The investigation must be original, non-biased, non-partisan and rigorously researched, with the decision based on how deserving the work is to be in the public domain. Named for David McClure, the news producer, author and journalist known for his forensic investigations into institutional privilege and the British Royal family's finances. APPLICATION: a 1,500-word pitch including a synopsis of the investigation and its aims, objectives, key research undertaken and progress to date, the support needed to complete it, and how the funds will be used. The fellow starts in September; the annual application deadline is 31 May. Fully remote.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Restless Egg: Luxury Technology Accelerator 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Restless Egg

Location: Remote

Award: $50,000 initial investment for 5% equity, with up to an additional $150,000 follow-on tied to performance milestones. Programme includes weekly check-ins, monthly crits, milestone-driven reviews, access to a network of subject matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors, curated events, and an artist-founder demo day. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month accelerator (programme begins September 2026)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Six-month Restless Egg accelerator for artist-founders building commercial products with AI and other emerging technologies under the theme of 'Luxury Technology': products that pluralise what AI makes possible across perception, pleasure, communication, ritual and creativity, rather than productivity, surveillance or 'slop'. ELIGIBILITY: artist-founders with an experimental, culturally attuned methodology; work that originates as obsession or frontier practice rather than as a product brief; clearly identified users and real demand; and potential to build a durable company (not a one-off artwork or pure research project). NOTE: this is an equity-based accelerator investment, not a grant - $50,000 initial in exchange for 5% equity, with follow-on conditional on hitting milestones.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BBQ+ Academic Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Black, Brown, and Queer Studies (BBQ+), supported by the Mellon Foundation

Location: Remote (fully virtual organisation; all fellowship meetings via Zoom with closed captioning)

Award: Tiered research stipend by academic level: College students $1,000; Master's/Early PhD $2,000; Advanced PhD $5,000; Recent postdoctoral scholars $10,000. Fellows may also apply to reimburse other research or professionalisation expenses.

Duration: Approximately mid-September 2026 through end of April 2027 (~8 months); weekly Friday meetings 12:00 to 14:00 ET

Deadline: Closed 01 May 2026

Annual academic fellowship for underrepresented scholars (broadly and intersectionally defined) working in critical race, Indigenous, postcolonial and queer studies. Open to college students (Sophomores and up, including community college students), recent graduates, Master's students and recent Master's holders (up to 3 years post-degree), PhD/EdD/other doctoral students, and recent postdoctoral scholars (up to 8 years post-degree). Parental, family, sick and similar leaves do not count toward years-post-degree. Open to undocumented and international applicants. Fellows are placed in one of four cohorts (Undergraduate, Master's & Early Doctoral, Advanced Doctoral/Dissertation, Postdoctoral) with a dedicated cohort advisor. Weekly meetings alternate among Writing Clinic, Theory Clinic, Colloquium and Fellows Circle. Application materials: 1-page cover letter, 1-2 page research proposal, CV, and academic writing sample of at least 8 pages of continuous prose. Deadline 1 May 2026 by midnight ET; review begins 4 May 2026; late applications unlikely to receive full consideration. Questions: fellowships@bbqplus.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rooted Futures Lab: Environmental Justice & Technology (EJIT) Fellowship - Summer 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Rooted Futures Lab

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: $3,000 USD stipend (flexible payment schedule)

Duration: ~12 weeks (15 June to 4 September 2026), ~20 hours/week (flexible)

Deadline: Closed 30 Apr 2026

Project-based summer fellowship for individuals working at the intersection of technology, infrastructure and environmental justice. Each Fellow contributes to a defined project area working independently and in close collaboration with Rooted Futures Lab staff and other Fellows. All tracks produce tangible outputs that support communities, advance critical inquiry and strengthen the broader EJIT field. Summer 2026 tracks: (1) Data Centers & Environmental Justice: Creative Technologist Fellow; (2) Data Centers & Environmental Justice: Investigative Storyteller Fellow; (3) Study Hall: Democratic Governance & Collective Systems Fellow; (4) GLITCH Zine: Editor & Strategist Fellow. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis; status notification within 3 weeks of the deadline. Inclusive selection: RFL does not discriminate on sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, race, ethnicity, religion, age or economic background, and explicitly encourages applicants from underrepresented identities. Inspired by Dr. Robert Bullard's environmental justice framework and Octavia Butler's histofuturism. Questions: info@rootedfutureslab.io (subject 'EJIT Fellowship Application').

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Kapor Foundation

Location: Remote (US-focused tech policy/research)

Award: Cash award (amount per project; first awards announced by 30 June 2026)

Duration: Per project

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Supports journalists producing long-form investigative reports and tech-policy researchers conducting research, analysis or evaluation that informs policy related to the Kapor Foundation's three priority areas, with an emphasis on responsible AI and tech ethics. Priority areas: CS/AI Education, Innovation, Governance. Aims to dismantle systemic inequities in the tech sector by funding researchers and investigative journalists exploring barriers and driving actionable solutions. Applications accepted on a rolling basis; first awards to be announced by 30 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships
item.description:
Organisation: OSV Fellowship

Location: Remote

Award: Up to $100,000

Duration: 1 year (remote)

Deadline: Closed 30 Apr 2026

Equity-free fellowship open to adults worldwide. No application fee. Includes access to investors, partners, mentors and optional in-person gatherings for Fellows.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Critical Playground: Freelance Writer Commissions
item.description:
Organisation: Critical Playground

Location: Remote

Award: Paid commissions

Duration: Per article (800 to 900 words)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Commissioned long-form writing at the intersection of design, technology, art and culture. Editorial themes: designing with AI as cultural and infrastructural system; responsive and adaptive materials; politics of platforms and creative-infrastructure governance; post-digital hybrid making; designing for collapse and continuity; creative research as practice. In-depth pieces only, no press releases or promotional copy.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Working-Class Creative Grant (Karim Boumjimar)
item.description:
Organisation: Karim Boumjimar (independent artist initiative)

Location: Online / Remote

Award: €500 (no strings attached)

Duration: One-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Independent artist-run grant redistributing a portion of Karim Boumjimar's artwork sales as a no-strings-attached 500 EUR award for working-class creatives anywhere in the world. Funds may be used for artistic production, research, materials, travel or basic living needs. Simple application: short intro and a sample of work or interests. No fee, no reporting, no obligation to produce. Rolling review based on available funds; non-selected applicants stay in the pool for future rounds.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
